---
title: "3.1 Abstraction and Program Design"
subtitle: "Class Creation"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit3-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### Program design starts before code

<span class="term">Object-oriented design</span> (面向对象设计) organizes programs around objects that store data and provide behaviors.

Before writing Java, decide:

::: {.tight-list}
- what objects the program needs
- what data each object stores
- what behaviors each object should provide
- how objects interact
:::

## Object-Oriented Design

### Identify nouns and verbs

A useful first pass is to read a problem statement:

::: {.tight-list}
- nouns often suggest classes or objects
- verbs often suggest methods
- descriptive facts often suggest instance variables
:::

Student response task: for a game with players, scores, and turns, list two possible classes and two behaviors each class should have.

## UML Class Diagrams

### A compact design sketch

![Turtle UML class diagram](../../assets/3-class-creation/3-1-abstraction-and-program-design/turtleUMLClassDiagram.png){fig-align="center" width="48%"}

A simple class diagram can show:

::: {.tight-list}
- class name
- instance variables
- constructors
- methods
:::

The diagram is a planning tool, not a full Java program.

## Abstraction

### Hide details so the important idea is visible

<span class="term">Abstraction</span> (抽象) means managing complexity by focusing on the important ideas and hiding unnecessary details.

In Java classes, abstraction appears when:

::: {.tight-list}
- an object exposes methods instead of internal implementation details
- a method name describes a task without showing every statement at the call site
- a class stores private data and offers controlled public behaviors
:::

## Data Abstraction

### Represent an object's state with selected variables

Data abstraction means choosing what information belongs in an object.

Example: a `Student` class may store:

```java
private String name;
private int gradeLevel;
private double gpa;
```

The class does not need to store every possible fact about a student. It stores the data needed by the program.

## Procedural Abstraction

### Replace repeated steps with a named method

Repeated version:

```java
System.out.println("Old MacDonald had a farm");
System.out.println("E-I-E-I-O");
System.out.println("And on that farm he had a cow");
```

Method version:

```java
public static void verse(String animal)
{
    System.out.println("Old MacDonald had a farm");
    System.out.println("E-I-E-I-O");
    System.out.println("And on that farm he had a " + animal);
}
```

## Code Task

### Use the method abstraction

Call the method for two animals.

```java
public class Song
{
    public static void verse(String animal)
    {
        System.out.println("Old MacDonald had a farm");
        System.out.println("E-I-E-I-O");
        System.out.println("And on that farm he had a " + animal);
    }

    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        verse("cow");
        verse("duck");
    }
}
```

## Group Challenge

### Game design

Design a small game using object-oriented thinking.

Your group should produce:

::: {.tight-list}
- two or more classes
- at least two instance variables for each class
- at least two behaviors for each class
- one sentence explaining how the objects interact
:::

Keep the design small enough that another group could start coding it.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- explain how object-oriented design uses objects, data, and behaviors
- identify likely classes, instance variables, and methods from a problem
- explain how a UML-style class diagram supports planning
- distinguish data abstraction from procedural abstraction
- explain how a method reduces repeated code
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
